mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20d return volatility in triggered periods is not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows, or individual series show negative correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above its 12-month moving average signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns over 60 days are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of sustained INDPRO growth (t-test p > 0.05) or INDPRO shows negative correlation with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in residential construction permits above 900,000 units signals strong demand for housing, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances when HOUST exceeds 900k, or HOUST shows no significant correlation with returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation rate below 2% signals stable price expectations, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns over 60 days are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of CPI < 2%, or CPI shows negative correlation with returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain exceeding 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through consumer spending optimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or PAYEMS shows no significant correlation with returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising real consumer spending (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates improving demand-side fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and UNRATE on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows, or individual series show negative correlation with returns.
